name: Labber
description: A commercial measurement and instrument-control environment used in research labs for automated experiment sequencing and data capture.
whyUseIt: Labber is valuable when the challenge is managing instruments and repeatable measurement flows rather than writing every layer of lab automation by hand.
fieldPosition: Lab experiment automation and readout workflows
engineerWorkflow: Configure instruments, define a measurement sequence, run automated sweeps, and export results for further analysis.
managerExpects: A measurement setup that is repeatable enough for calibration, comparison, and team handoff.
demoDescription: Set up a repeatable mock experiment flow with parameter sweeps and review how the captured data supports later analysis.
beginnerMistakes: Treating a GUI setup as self-documenting when the measurement logic is still implicit
Not validating the instrument sequence before long runs
